GHSA-R6GQ-WHWQ-MVG9.896a8ee981d8089b9edc2309aa10dcac6e2dec48:
Add realpath symlink check to FileSystemPathPointer.join()

After the existing lexical normpath check, resolve symlinks with
realpath() and verify the result stays within the corpus root.
This prevents symlink escapes where a path looks lexically
contained but resolves outside the root via a symlink.

Also blocks absolute file IDs.

Fixes GHSA-r6gq-whwq-mvg9. Does not touch open() — that is
handled by pathsec.open() via _secure_open.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
GHSA-R6GQ-WHWQ-MVG9.cbe04c348677ee0d80dd4753d602d5915faad95f:
Unconditionally enforce path containment in FileSystemPathPointer

FileSystemPathPointer is the trust boundary for corpus readers.
Its open() and join() methods must always enforce containment
regardless of pathsec.ENFORCE setting.

Changes:
- Add _check_containment(): resolves symlinks with realpath() and
  verifies the path is within an allowed nltk.data.path directory.
  Always raises ValueError on violation (not warn-only).
- open(): calls _check_containment() before opening, fixing
  GHSA-72r2-7mfr-5xr9 (no-op sandbox check)
- join(): adds realpath-based symlink resolution check after the
  lexical normpath check, fixing GHSA-r6gq-whwq-mvg9 (symlink
  escape). Also blocks absolute file IDs.

These checks are independent of ENFORCE=True/False because they
protect the corpus reader sandbox, not general file I/O.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

GHSA-RF74-V2FM-23PW.00cdcd392142e6c745e7120c8d50a24127df5fad:
Fix GHSA-rf74-v2fm-23pw: unbounded recursion in JSONTaggedDecoder

JSONTaggedDecoder.decode_obj() recursed without depth limit,
allowing a crafted deeply-nested JSON payload to trigger
RecursionError and crash the process (denial of service).

Fix:
- Add MAX_DECODE_DEPTH = 200 class attribute as hard limit
- Add _depth counter parameter to decode_obj() that raises
  ValueError when exceeded
- Catch RecursionError in decode() for payloads that exceed
  Python's C-level json parser recursion limit (~1000) before
  decode_obj is reached

No other recursive JSON decoding paths exist in the codebase.
The stdlib json.load/json.loads calls in data.py, help.py,
sentiment/util.py, twitter/common.py, and tag/perceptron.py
do not use JSONTaggedDecoder and are not affected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
